---
title: "GlobalFoundries Stuffing Customers With High Inventory"
date: "2022-08-12T16:31:54.659Z"
url: "https://newsletter.semianalysis.com/p/globalfoundries-stuffing-customers"
author: "Dylan Patel"
description: "We are bearish on GlobalFoundries."
---

GlobalFoundries recently had their capital markets date, so we want to share our take on it. While the sell-side bankers are incredibly bullish, and the stock has gone up 20% over this week, we want to share our bearish view. We have even owned and recommended GlobalFoundries, but we are now a seller. As a side note, we have written extensively about them in the [past, including capacity, expansion, and technology](https://semianalysis.substack.com/p/globalfoundries-gfs-ipo-mubadala). We have said they are leading edge in specialty technologies, and in particular, we have praised [their RF-SOI](https://semianalysis.substack.com/p/qualcomm-rffe-update-8b-by-2025-2023) and [silicon photonics “Fotonix” offerings](https://semianalysis.substack.com/p/globalfoundries-fotonix-the-leading). Our content [was even included as a slide in the CTO’s capital markets day presentation this week](https://twitter.com/semiairlines/status/1557368706837118976?s=20&t=i-OQLBhyo2yO3wyXYOzqzg).

Today we want to talk about how they are stuffing their customers with inventory, why their long-term commitments aren’t as rosy as they proclaim, why the geopolitical valuation argument holds very little water, and why we are negative on the firm’s stock.
